mnemonics.title: Cloud Pricing Models
mnemonics.content:
SPOT: S - Save money, P - Pay less, O - Opportunistic, T - Temporary
OR: O - On-demand (flexible), R - Reserved (commitment = discount)

mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Steps
mnemonics.content: FORECAST: F - Forecast demand, O - Optimize resources, R - Right-size, E - Evaluate utilization, C - Calculate costs, A - Adjust dynamically, S - Scale appropriately, T - Track metrics

acronyms.usage: Contract specifying guaranteed uptime (e.g., 99.9%) and performance metrics
Cloud model providing virtualized computing resources over the internet
Cloud model providing development platforms and tools without managing infrastructure
Cloud model delivering software applications over the internet on subscription basis
Metric measuring profitability of cloud investments relative to costs
Complete cost including acquisition, operation, and maintenance of cloud resources

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a movie theater with pre-sold tickets - unused seats cannot be sold to others and revenue is lost. Reserved cloud instances work similarly - paid capacity unused is wasted.
Think of an accordion expanding and contracting - cloud resources automatically expand during high demand and shrink during low demand to match needs exactly.
Balance scale: left side = cost (over-provisioning = wasted money), right side = performance (under-provisioning = poor service). Optimal is balance in middle.
Two shopping scenarios: Reserved = buying annual gym membership (discounted, must use), On-Demand = pay-per-visit (expensive but flexible).

analogies.analogy: Like a prepaid data plan with monthly data limit - unused megabytes don't roll over to next month, they're simply lost.

analogies.analization: Like a hotel that automatically adds or removes floors based on booking demand - never too empty, never too full.

analogies.analogy: Like wearing shoes - too big (over-provisioning) wastes material, too small (under-provisioning) causes pain. Perfect fit (right-sizing) is optimal.
Like airline standby tickets - available at deep discounts but may not get a seat if demand is high; risk of being bumped.
Like having accounts at multiple banks - not dependent on one, can compare rates, and diversify risk.
Like a car fuel efficiency - a car sitting in driveway (low utilization) wastes the investment; high utilization means getting full value.
